significant_events.event_name: Subvention to Animal Welfare Organisations Program
significant_events.source_quote: During the year ended 31 March 2022, the Charity received funding support of HK$129,930 (2021: HK$150,000) from the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department under the scheme named "Subvention to Animal Welfare Organisations". The fund was used for the education programe and the nutritional supplements and veterinary medical costs for rehoming rabbits.
significant_events.summary: Received HK$129,930 government funding for education, nutritional supplements, and veterinary medical costs for rehoming rabbits.

significant_events.source_quote: HKRS aims to flight for living rights, respects and concerns for rabbits and also to minimize the number of abandonment. We hold campaigns and activities to promote knowledge and responsibilities of raising rabbits, caring to animal, respects of lives so that we can build a harmonious community between humans and animals.
significant_events.summary: Held campaigns and activities to promote knowledge and responsibilities of raising rabbits, animal care, and respect for lives.

significant_events.source_quote: We are now recruiting new volunteers to have a greater help force on the increasing number of abandoned rabbits. Volunteers would be responsible for cage-cleaning, general care, foster family and other promotion campaign.
significant_events.summary: Recruiting volunteers for cage-cleaning, general care, foster family support, and promotion campaigns for abandoned rabbits.

significant_events.source_quote: We have already obtained the correct license from the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conversation Department to provide a boarding service for animals in our care.
significant_events.summary: Obtained license from Agriculture, Fisheries and Conservation Department to provide boarding services for animals in their care.

data.original_text: Applicants must be above the age of 18. Adoption applications are only valid for HKSAR permanent residents living in Hong Kong. Any applications from organizations or groups will not be accepted.
Only for applicants aged 12 or above. Applicants who is aged 12 to under 16 should be accompained by an adult who is at least 18 years old.
We welcome people from all sectors to register in this new voluntary reward program namely . This is an opportunity for you to help those abandoned rabbits.
HKRS aims to flight for living rights, respects and concerns for rabbits and also to minimize the number of abandonment.

HKRS aims to flight for living rights, respects and concerns for rabbits and also to minimize the number of abandonment. We hold campaigns and activities to promote knowledge and responsibilities of raising rabbits, caring to animal, respects of lives so that we can build a harmonious community between humans and animals.

We are now recruiting new volunteers to have a greater help force on the increasing number of abandoned rabbits. Volunteers would be responsible for cage-cleaning, general care, foster family and other promotion campaign.
The HKRS finances all it's operations through the selling of merchandise, providing different services and donations from the public.
We have already obtained the correct license from the Agriculture, Fisheries and Conversation Department to provide a boarding service for animals in our care.

The Hong Kong Rabbit Society's Annual Report (2015 – 2016) focused more on introducing their work, the knowledge and experiences about taking care of rabbits. ... The content of its annual report includes the following items: ... Work report: including seeking sponsorship, organizing lectures and visits, the number of abandoned and successfully adopted rabbits, media interview records and shooting promotional videos, etc.

meta.chinese_name: 香港兔友協會有限公司
